description: The simplest and perhaps most profound tantric position: partners lie completely still, face-to-face, fully connected but without any physical movement at all. All focus is directed inward toward sensation, breath, heartbeat, and the felt sense of union.

howTo.text: Both partners lie face-to-face on their sides, or with Partner B gently on top of Partner A.
Connect fully but do not move. Close eyes and breathe slowly.
Focus entirely on internal sensations: heartbeat, warmth, subtle involuntary movements, and the presence of your partner.

tips: This practice can last from five minutes to over an hour
The stillness often reveals sensations that movement masks
physicalNotes: Zero physical demand. The only requirement is patience and the willingness to be fully present.

name: The Backstroke
description: Partner A lies on their back. Partner B sits on top facing Partner A's feet, then leans all the way back until lying on Partner A's chest. This creates a full back-to-chest contact with a reversed orientation.

howTo.text: Partner A lies flat on their back.
Partner B sits on top facing away (toward Partner A's feet).
Partner B slowly leans back until they are lying on Partner A's chest, face-up, stacked directly on top.

tips: Partner A can wrap arms around Partner B's torso once they are reclined
Partner B can use their feet on the bed for leverage to create movement
physicalNotes: Partner A bears Partner B's weight. Comfortable if partners are similar in size. Partner B uses leg muscles for movement.

name: The Cradle Rock
description: Both partners lie on their sides facing each other. Partner A cradles Partner B's head on their arm while the other arm wraps around Partner B's waist. Legs intertwine gently. The cradling aspect adds a deeply nurturing quality.

howTo.text: Both partners lie on their sides facing each other.
Partner A extends their lower arm for Partner B to rest their head on, like a pillow.
Legs intertwine naturally, and Partner A's upper arm wraps around Partner B's waist while both rock gently.

tips: Place a pillow under Partner A's lower arm to prevent it from falling asleep
This is one of the most emotionally nurturing positions available
physicalNotes: Minimal physical demand. Partner A's lower arm may experience numbness over time; a pillow helps.

name: The Bouncer
description: Partner A sits on a large exercise or stability ball while Partner B sits on their lap facing them or facing away. The ball's natural bounce and instability add a dynamic, playful element to seated positions.

howTo.text: Partner A sits on a fully inflated stability ball, feet wide apart on the floor for balance.
Partner B sits on Partner A's lap, either facing them or facing away.
Both use the ball's natural bounce to create movement, with Partner A's feet providing ground stability.

tips: Ensure the ball is properly inflated and rated for your combined weight
Position the ball against a wall for added stability, especially when starting out
physicalNotes: Requires good core balance from Partner A. The ball introduces instability that demands constant micro-adjustments.

name: The Side Plank
description: Both partners lie on their sides facing the same direction, like spooning, but Partner B props up on one elbow and lifts their upper body. Partner A lifts their upper leg. This elevated spooning allows for deeper access and more vigorous movement.

howTo.text: Both partners lie on the same side in a spooning arrangement, Partner A in front.
Partner B props up on their elbow, lifting their upper body off the bed.
Partner A lifts their upper leg, and both use the elevated positioning for more dynamic movement.

tips: Partner B's supporting elbow should be on a padded surface
This is essentially an energized version of spooning
physicalNotes: Moderate arm fatigue for Partner B from the propped position. Transitions naturally from and back to standard spooning.

name: The Hammock Swing
description: Partners lie in a gentle embrace, either face-to-face or spooned, and create a slow, swinging motion as if they are in a hammock. The swaying, rocking movement induces deep relaxation and a trance-like intimacy.

howTo.text: Both partners settle into a comfortable lying embrace, either facing or spooned.
Together, create a slow, gentle full-body rocking motion, as though swaying in a hammock.
Let the rhythm slow naturally, breathing deeply and letting the sway become hypnotic.

tips: A waterbed or memory foam mattress enhances the swaying sensation
Let the motion become slower and slower over time for a deepening effect
physicalNotes: Nearly zero physical demand. The motion should be as effortless as possible.
